reachability_artifacts: cli-run: go test -count=1 -v -run 'TestRunSliceDispatchesAllLegsViaRegistry|TestRunSliceResolutionFailure|TestRunSliceCaptainResolutionFailureDefersAndProceeds' ./internal/run/ — RunSlice drives all three role legs through a registry-injected fake driver; resolution failures error/defer with zero dispatches — see proof.json reachability.evidence

cli-run: go test -count=1 -v -run TestProxyRoutingAdvertisedEqualsActual ./internal/driver/registry/ — R-04 three-part artefact: advertise / server-side-observed proxy dispatch / SWORN_DIRECT flips both surfaces
internal/run/slice_terminal_test.go:TestTerminalError_AllKinds (D3 terminal set {auth, credits} at the implement leg)
internal/verify/verify_agentic_test.go:TestRunAgenticTerminalDispatchErrorBlocked (D3 terminal set at the verify leg)

effort_complexity.rationale: Largest seam rewire: three role legs, verify transport swap with semantics preservation, factory deletion, import-boundary contract.

design_decisions.choice: D1 — Registry injection: RunSliceOptions.Registry *registry.Registry, nil -> registry.Default(model.ProviderConfigFromEnv()); the NewAgent/NewVerifier factory fields are deleted (not deprecated) from RunSliceOptions AND run.Options, and tests inject fake drivers through a test registry (registry.New() + Register), never through factories.
options: registry field with nil->Default (chosen) — one seam, cmd call sites compile unchanged
keep factory fields alongside the registry — rejected: preserves the nil-factory SIGSEGV class AC-01 exists to delete
design_decisions.human_decision: Brad, 2026-07-02, planning session (intake registry/resolution decisions); acknowledged clean in the Captain review (review.md 2026-07-10 §2) and the Coach acknowledgement (captain-proceed.md 2026-07-10).
design_decisions.rationale: The registry's role check subsumes the deleted newAgentFromModel CapChat gate ('capability IS the role set', S05 design intent); no code path can hold an unresolved driver.

design_decisions.choice: D2 — RoleCaptain declared by the in-process drivers only; a captain-leg Resolve failure records the registry's descriptive role error as a durable Rule 2 deferral via recordDesignGateDeferral (sworn#51) and proceeds — AC-02's hard-error contract applies to the implement and verify legs. AC-02's text was AMENDED on release-wt (@8ff95cc) so spec and code agree; this narrowing is decided, not drifted.
options: (i) fail-open deferral for the captain leg, hard error for implement/verify (chosen, with AC-02 amendment)
(ii) hard-fail all three legs per AC-02's original literal text — rejected: bricks every subprocess-first run at the design gate since no subprocess driver declares RoleCaptain
design_decisions.human_decision: Brad (Coach), 2026-07-10, S06 captain-proceed.md pin 1 (escalate pin RATIFIED live): deferral reading stands, AC-02 amended, follow-up restoring role-universality (RoleCaptain on subprocess drivers) filed as sworn#86.
design_decisions.rationale: A captain gate that cannot run defers loudly and the run proceeds (the established recordDesignGateDeferral contract); an unserved implement/verify dispatch is a wrong-answer path and must hard-error before any model dispatch.

design_decisions.choice: D3 — One terminal-kind predicate in the contract package: driver.TerminalErrKind with the set exactly {auth, credits} (driver.ErrKindAuth, driver.ErrKindCredits — the latter promoted from inprocess's private errKindCredits), consumed at BOTH the implement leg (replacing model.IsTerminal) and the verify leg's terminal->BLOCKED mapping. Closes the S04 D5 Rule-2 tracked obligation on this slice.
options: single contract predicate consumed at both points (chosen)
auth-only check — rejected: silently loses the credits fail-fast (subprocess drivers collapse terminal cases to auth; the in-process driver emits credits as its own kind)
per-consumer hand-synced sets — rejected: the drift R-03 names
design_decisions.human_decision: Brad (Coach), 2026-07-10, T3 captain-proceed.md (S04 acknowledgement — the binding record for the {auth, credits} set) and S06 captain-proceed.md pin 3 (citation carried into the TerminalErrKind doc comment and this record).
design_decisions.rationale: A terminal driver error can never succeed on retry or model escalation; one predicate at both consumption points means the fail-fast property cannot split across the transport swap.

design_decisions.choice: D6 — Single proxy predicate model.ProxyRoute extracted from FromEnv; model.ResolveLoopClient is the in-process drivers' FromEnv-equivalent client default (replacing proxy-blind model.NewClient); registry.proxyRouting delegates to the same predicate. Binding R-04 artefact: three-part reachability test (capabilities advertise ViaProxy / dispatch observed server-side at the proxy / SWORN_DIRECT=1 flips both surfaces together). SWORN_PROXY_URL stays test-only per the credential-trust boundary.
options: extract one predicate behind FromEnv + ResolveLoopClient + registry (chosen)

keep registry-side duplicate of the login condition — rejected: capabilities would claim proxy while registry-dispatched execution goes direct, regressing the keyless-credits (S06b/OpenRouter) journey
design_decisions.human_decision: Brad (Coach), 2026-07-10, S06 captain-proceed.md pin 4 (CONFIRMED, proceed as designed; constitutional flag acknowledged — D6/D7 change credential-based routing).
design_decisions.rationale: Enumeration and dispatch must evaluate literally the same function so the capability surface can never lie about the dispatch route; protects the memory-validated aggregator/proxy journey.

design_decisions.choice: D7 — ProviderConfigFromEnv widened so every provider key reads envOrAlias(CANONICAL, SWORN_CANONICAL): canonical wins, SWORN_* becomes a fallback everywhere, keeping SWORN_*-only worker setups on direct dispatch when the default registry is built from ProviderConfigFromEnv. Strictly additive, proven by a canonical-wins precedence test covering every widened key.
options: widen with envOrAlias for every provider key (chosen)
leave only OPENAI/GOOGLE aliased — rejected: a SWORN_OPENROUTER_API_KEY-only environment (the documented worker setup) would silently lose direct dispatch at the S06 cutover
design_decisions.human_decision: Brad (Coach), 2026-07-10, S06 captain-proceed.md pin 5 (ACCEPTED with the mandatory per-key canonical-wins precedence test; behaviour outside the literal touchpoints contained by the track merging as one unit).
design_decisions.rationale: The default registry must be built from the same config sworn capabilities uses or the R-04 predicate splits on the key half; canonical-wins keeps the widening backward-compatible.

open_deferrals.item: RoleCaptain on the subprocess drivers (claude-cli, codex) — a subprocess-first escalation head routes its captain leg through the fail-open Rule 2 deferral path instead of a served dispatch
open_deferrals.why: claude -p is an edit-capable loop that cannot be made read-only this slice; no AC requires subprocess captain dispatch — AC-02 was amended to the deferral contract
open_deferrals.acknowledgement: Escalate pin ratified live: fail-open deferral reading stands, AC-02 amended on release-wt, follow-up filed as sworn#86 (S06 captain-proceed.md pin 1)
